back_translation: Then Solomon said, “The LORD has said that He would dwell in the thick cloud.
But I have built an exalted house for You, a place for You to dwell forever.”
Then the king turned and blessed the whole assembly of Israel while they were standing.
He said, “Blessed be the LORD, the God of Israel, who with His hand has fulfilled what He spoke with His mouth to David my father, saying,

‘Since the day I brought My people out of the land of Egypt, I have not chosen a city from any of the tribes of Israel to build a house for My name to be there, nor have I chosen anyone to be ruler over My people Israel.
But I have chosen Jerusalem for My name to be there, and I have chosen David to be over My people Israel.’
It was in the heart of David my father to build a house for the name of the LORD, the God of Israel.
But the LORD said to David my father, ‘Since it was in your heart to build a house for My name, you did well that this was in your heart.
Nevertheless, you shall not be the one to build the house; but your son, who will come from you, he shall build the house for My name.’

Now the LORD has fulfilled the word He spoke. I have risen up in place of David my father, and I sit on the throne of Israel, as the LORD promised, and I have built the house for the name of the LORD, the God of Israel.
And there I have set the ark, in which is the covenant of the LORD that He made with the Israelites.”
Then Solomon stood before the altar of the LORD in front of the whole assembly of Israel and spread out his hands.

For Solomon had made a bronze platform, five cubits long, five cubits wide, and three cubits high, and set it in the middle of the courtyard; he stood on it, then knelt down before the whole assembly of Israel and spread out his hands toward heaven,
and said, “O LORD, God of Israel, there is no God like You in heaven or on earth, who keeps covenant and steadfast love with Your servants who walk before You with all their heart;
who has kept what You promised to Your servant David my father; what You spoke with Your mouth You have fulfilled with Your hand this day.

Therefore now, O LORD, God of Israel, keep what You promised to Your servant David my father, when You said, ‘You shall not lack a man to sit before Me on the throne of Israel, if only your sons guard their way to walk in My law as you have walked before Me.’
And now, O LORD, God of Israel, let the word that You spoke to Your servant David be confirmed.
But will God indeed dwell with man on the earth? Behold, heaven and the highest heaven cannot contain You; how much less this house that I have built!
Yet regard the prayer and the plea of Your servant, O LORD my God, and hear the cry and the prayer that Your servant prays before You,
that Your eyes may watch over this house day and night, toward the place where You said You would put Your name, to hear the prayer that Your servant prays toward this place.
And hear the pleas of Your servant and of Your people Israel when they pray toward this place; hear from heaven, Your dwelling place; and when You hear, forgive.
When a man sins against his neighbor and is required to take an oath, and he comes to swear before Your altar in this house,

then hear from heaven and act, and judge Your servants, condemning the wicked by bringing his way on his own head, and declaring the righteous innocent by rewarding him according to his righteousness.
When Your people Israel are defeated before an enemy because they have sinned against You, and they turn back to You and confess Your name and pray and plead before You in this house,
then hear from heaven and forgive the sin of Your people Israel, and bring them back to the land that You gave to them and to their fathers.
When the heavens are shut and there is no rain because they have sinned against You, if they pray toward this place and confess Your name and turn from their sin when You afflict them,

then hear from heaven and forgive the sin of Your servants, Your people Israel, teaching them the good way in which they should walk; and send rain on Your land that You gave to Your people as an inheritance.
If there is famine in the land, if there is plague, blight, mildew, locusts, or young locusts, if their enemies besiege them in the cities of their land, whatever plague or sickness comes,
whatever prayer or plea anyone or all Your people Israel make—each knowing his own affliction and pain, and spreading out his hands toward this house—
then hear from heaven, Your dwelling place, and forgive, and give to each according to all his ways, whose heart You know (for You alone know the hearts of all the children of men),
so that they may fear You and walk in Your ways all the days that they live in the land that You gave to our fathers.

Likewise, concerning the foreigner who is not of Your people Israel but comes from a distant land because of Your great name and Your mighty hand and Your outstretched arm—when he comes and prays toward this house,

then hear from heaven, Your dwelling place, and do according to all that the foreigner calls to You for, so that all the peoples of the earth may know Your name and fear You as Your people Israel do, and may know that this house I have built is called by Your name.
When Your people go out to war against their enemies, by whatever way You send them, and they pray to You toward this city that You have chosen and the house I have built for Your name,
then hear from heaven their prayer and their plea, and uphold their cause.
When they sin against You—for there is no one who does not sin—and You are angry with them and give them over to an enemy, so they are taken captive to a land far or near,
if they take it to heart in the land to which they were taken captive, and repent and plead with You in the land of their captors, saying, ‘We have sinned, we have done wrong and acted wickedly,’

if they turn back to You with all their heart and all their soul in the land of the enemies who took them captive, and pray toward the land You gave to their fathers, the city You have chosen, and the house I have built for Your name,
then hear from heaven, Your dwelling place, their prayer and their pleas, and uphold their cause; and forgive Your people who have sinned against You.
Now, my God, let Your eyes be open and Your ears attentive to the prayer offered in this place.
Now therefore, O LORD God, arise and enter Your resting place, You and the ark of Your might. Let Your priests, O LORD God, be clothed with salvation, and let Your godly ones rejoice in goodness.
O LORD God, do not reject the one You have anointed; remember the steadfast love promised to David Your servant.”
